content:
# Recommendations ‚Äî after iter-079

## CRITICAL ‚Äî the route that just unblocked
- **Co-dispatch TensorObjInverse.lean {1128, 1186, 1222, 1990}** (S3 / S4a / (b) / telescope). Route
  c.2 (`presheafDualPullbackComparison_restrict`) is CLOSED, so PresheafDualPullback.lean is a green
  sibling and the import-chain build race (ARCHON_MEMORY iter-029) no longer forces SOLO on the dual
  flank. These 4 are the next frontier. Verify readiness against the green sibling before dispatch;
  S3/S4a consume the now-closed c.2 + Œ∏-unit `presheafDualUnitIso_naturality`, (b)
  `sheafificationCompPullback_restrict`, telescope = exposition assembly.
- Was deferred SOLO across many iters specifically *because* c.2 was open. That gate is gone.

## HIGH ‚Äî blueprint reconciliation (lvb `presheafdualpullback-iter079`, 3 major, all blueprint-side)
Dispatch a **blueprint-writer** on `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`, label
`lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict`:
1. **Drop `\uses` edges** `lem:slice_dual_transport_comp` and `lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom`
   from both `\uses` blocks (L8212, L8250) ‚Äî neither is called by the realized close.
2. **Add a blueprint block** for `PresheafOfModules.dual_map_app_apply` (used 3√ó in the close; `:= rfl`
   value lemma) and for **`hstar_naturality`** (the new œÜ-naturality keystone; body
   `(naturality_apply œÜ g z).symm`; `\uses{PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply}`). Both feed
   `lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict`.
3. **Rewrite the close-route prose** (L8349‚Äì8356 region) from the `slice_dual_transport_comp` /
   `Subsingleton.elim` narrative to the realized **thin-poset `homOfLE ‚â° eqToHom` defeq + `convert key
   using 2`** form. The review `% NOTE` at ~L8252 already records the target shape for the writer.
- Note: `slice_dual_transport_comp` (SliceTransport.lean) remains a valid sorry-free lemma in the repo;
  it is simply not on this lemma's critical path. Do not delete it; just remove the false `\uses` edge.

## MEDIUM ‚Äî comment hygiene (lean-auditor `iter079`, non-blocking)
- L1082 close comment reads `CLOSE (iter-080):` ‚Äî off-by-one; should be iter-079. The prover has a
  documented iter-label off-by-one habit (also flagged iter-078). Fix opportunistically when a prover
  next edits the file; do NOT spend a dedicated lane.
- L312‚Äì313 planner FALLBACK comment says "(as here)" implying the body is a `sorry`; the body is
  complete. Stale. Same opportunistic fix.

## MEDIUM ‚Äî 1:1 coverage debt
- `archon dag-query unmatched` = **121** `lean_aux` nodes (was ~120). New this iter:
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.hstar_naturality`. Standing backlog of prover-helpers with no
  blueprint entry (`c2_assemble`, `cocycle_assemble`, `adj_unit_map_counit`, `dual_map_app_apply`, the
  `ppadj_*` value lemmas, ‚Ä¶). Roll the highest-leverage ones into the HIGH blueprint-writer round above;
  the rest are low-priority. Planner: when there is Lean there should be tex.

## Do NOT re-assign (dead ends, this iter)
- `erw [restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app]` on the c.2 RHS reindex ‚Äî wrong abstraction level (forget‚ÇÇ
  `Hom.app` vs ModuleCat `.val.app`) + `pushforward‚ÇÄ.map.app`/`forget.mapIso` wrapper mismatch.
- `rw [show RHS_z = M.val.map (eqToHom).op from rfl]` ‚Äî the reindex is semilinear (restrictScalars
  carrier diamond); no clean plain-`M.val.map` rfl form exists.
- A `hstar_naturality`-style helper that builds the slice morphism `g` *internally* from a `‚â§`
  hypothesis ‚Äî `convert` cannot unify the resulting coupled slices (leaves `convert_1/convert_2`). The
  morphism must be a DECOUPLED explicit argument supplied concretely at the call site.

## Reusable patterns discovered (added to PROJECT_STATUS Knowledge Base)
- **Thin-poset proof-irrelevance close.** In `Over (Opens X)` (and any thin poset / proof-irrelevant
  hom category), `homOfLE _ ‚â° eqToHom _` definitionally, so map-reindexes built from `eqToIso`/`eqToHom`
  are DEFEQ to the category's `.map g` for the unique `g`. A œÜ-naturality goal across such reindexes
  closes by `convert key using 2` (rfl-level) with NO `Subsingleton` side goals ‚Äî provided the naturality
  helper takes the morphism as a DECOUPLED explicit argument (coupling the endpoints into the morphism
  defeats `convert`'s slice unification).
